title: identity — subprocessors
description: Two categories of subprocessor. KMS provider for MFA-secret envelope encryption at rest (invoked on every enrolment + verification). haveibeenpwned for password-breach k-anonymity check (invoked on every password set / reset, optional per config). Email delivery is a subprocessor of `notifications`, not `identity` directly.

subprocessors.name: AWS KMS
subprocessors.purpose: Envelope encryption of Identity.mfa_secret_encrypted. Every MFA enrolment generates a fresh data key via KMS; encrypted data key stored inline with the ciphertext.
subprocessors.location: customer's AWS region (default us-east-1; configurable per deployment)
subprocessors.notes: Alternative backend: HashiCorp Vault (equivalent envelope-encryption workflow) for on-prem deployments. Local-file backend (config value 'local') is DEV-ONLY — the health probe refuses production boot when this is selected.

subprocessors.name: HashiCorp Vault
subprocessors.purpose: Envelope encryption alternative to AWS KMS for on-prem deployments. Same operational contract.

subprocessors.name: haveibeenpwned
subprocessors.purpose: K-anonymity password-breach check. We send the SHA-1 hash of the plaintext password truncated to the first 5 hex characters; HIBP returns a list of matching hash suffixes with breach counts. The plaintext + full hash NEVER leave the app.
data_processed: SHA-1 prefix (5 hex chars, 20 bits of entropy — not sufficient to reconstruct the password)
subprocessors.location: Cloudflare edge / Have I Been Pwned infrastructure
subprocessors.when_engaged: config('identity.password.breach_check_enabled') === true AND `password_breach_check` entitlement active for the tenant of the referring User
subprocessors.notes: The k-anonymity protocol is HIBP's native design — no per-user identifier is transmitted. HIBP has no visibility into which identity submitted the check. Rate-limited to 1 request per second per IP; we cache the truncated-hash response for 24 hours to survive HIBP outages.

not_a_subprocessor: Argon2id / bcrypt — in-process libraries, no data leaves the app
OpenTelemetry / metrics collector — receives aggregate metrics only (no identifiers, per metrics.json)
Email delivery (SES / Postmark / SMTP relay) — handled by `notifications` module; identity does not maintain its own delivery pipeline
